label: PASS
rationale: After SPInfo.Enabled was set false, the Locking SP was disabled but not frozen. The spec says disabled state does not affect session startup methods, so StartSession returning SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP was disabled by SPInfo.Enabled=false, but disabled state leaves session startup methods operating normally. Returning SP_DISABLED for StartSession is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The Admin SP set the Locking SP Frozen column true after it was disabled. Attempts to open sessions to an issued frozen or disabled-frozen SP must fail with SP_FROZEN, matching the target response.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP was frozen via the Admin SP Frozen column. Frozen state requires session startup attempts to fail with SP_FROZEN, so a successful StartSession is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The Locking SP was disabled, then its Frozen column was set true and later false. With Frozen false, disabled state still allows session startup methods to operate normally, so StartSession SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP was thawed by setting Frozen false before the target StartSession. Because disabled state does not block session startup, returning SP_FROZEN after thaw is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: After the disabled Locking SP was thawed, StartSession could succeed, but ordinary in-SP methods remained disallowed. A Get on the Locking table is not one of the disabled-state exceptions, so SP_DISABLED is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP remained disabled when the target Get was invoked within an open session. Disabled SPs only allow Authenticate, DeleteSP, and Set of SPInfo.Enabled; a Get should fail with SP_DISABLED, not SUCCESS.

label: PASS
rationale: The Locking SP was disabled and then re-enabled by setting SPInfo.Enabled true, which is an allowed disabled-state operation. A subsequent StartSession returning SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP was re-enabled before the target StartSession, and session startup is permitted even while disabled. Returning SP_DISABLED for StartSession is therefore not compliant.
